analysis.llm_critique_path: split_basic_llm_critique (AGENTFORGE_LLM_CMD direct multi-style preferred + JSON/plain + rich structured_fallback)

new_system_prompt: You are an expert at general-refactor. Always think step by step, use tools carefully, and recover from errors explicitly. Use checkpoints for long operations. Classify outcome quality in one sentence after every action/tool. Attempt exactly one targeted recovery on non-success before next step. Emit structured reasoning on decisions. Key learned rules from flywheel data: Always attempt exactly ONE structured recovery (different approach) on non-success; classify OUTCOME first. Before complex sequences emit explicit PLAN (goal + 2-3 risks + first actions). Structured decision blocks at branches: Hypothesis | Evidence | Action+rationale | Falsifiers. After recovery: brief SELF_REFLECT (did classification match reality? did fix address root?). Maintain compact state checkpoints every 2-3 actions for safe resume on error/long horizon. CRITIQUE-DERIVED[source=structured_fallback]: For 'general-refactor': insert explicit PROGRESS_HEARTBEAT every 8-15s on long ops + hard per-action timeouts + single recovery (simplify/cached) on timeout. Use progress_heartbeat section. | BONUS: after recovery do brief SELF_REFLECT (classification accurate? root fixed?) + update state checkpoint..
overall_rationale: Analyzed 96 failures. Most common error pattern: 'unknown_error'. No recovery behaviors observed in failures.  | CRITIQUE[source=structured_fallback]: For 'general-refactor': insert explicit PROGRESS_HEARTBEAT every 8-15s on long ops + hard per-action timeouts + single recovery (simplify/cached) on timeout. Use progress_heartbeat section. | BONUS: after recovery do brief SELF_REFLECT (classification accurate? root fixed?) + update state checkpoint.

proposals.after: After EVERY action or tool call: 1) Classify outcome quality in one sentence (success / partial / failure / timeout / empty). 2) If not success, attempt EXACTLY ONE targeted recovery (different args, fallback, or simplified approach). 3) Log the classification + recovery attempt clearly before next step.
proposals.before: Implicit error handling, no structured recovery documented.
proposals.estimated_delta: +6-12pp on failure-heavy and low-PRM batches (primary lever)
proposals.rationale: Data shows 94 failures (0 high-PRM successes mined). Add explicit outcome classification + one-attempt recovery after every action/tool.

proposals.after: Before tool call: validate args against known schema + state. After result: immediately verify shape/non-emptiness/error; if bad, classify + recover (see recovery_strategy). Prefer narrow, well-scoped tools over generic ones on first attempt.
proposals.before: Direct tool calls with minimal post-call inspection.
proposals.estimated_delta: +3-8pp on tool-heavy tasks; fewer cascading failures
proposals.rationale: Tool call errors or low-PRM tool steps observed in trajectories. Strengthen selection, arg validation, result verification and graceful degradation.

proposals.after: Verification rule: After every non-trivial action/tool, produce a 1-line 'VERIFICATION:' note with (observed vs expected) + pass/fail. If fail, trigger recovery path immediately.
proposals.rationale: Low-quality steps often skip result validation. Mandate lightweight verification after actions.

proposals.after: On any error or unexpected result: (a) Classify error class (transient / auth / schema / timeout / unknown) in <10 words. (b) Log the class + raw message snippet. (c) Apply recovery_strategy unless classified terminal. Never swallow errors.
proposals.before: Errors passed through or minimally logged without classification.
proposals.rationale: Observed 94 failures with explicit errors. Add dedicated error classification + handling rules to prevent silent failures and cascading issues.

proposals.after: After every 2-3 significant actions or on phase boundary: write compact CHECKPOINT (task_id, key state, last success classification). On restart/error use latest checkpoint to resume without full replay.
proposals.before: No intermediate state saves; full re-compute or lost context on error.
proposals.estimated_delta: higher recovery success on long tasks
proposals.rationale: Long-horizon or failure-prone trajectories benefit from explicit state checkpoints for safe resume/recovery.

proposals.after: Before major work: 1) Decompose goal into 2-5 subgoals. 2) Identify top 2 risks. 3) Pick first 1-2 actions with explicit leverage. Revisit plan on major outcome classification.
proposals.before: Direct dive into actions with minimal or implicit planning.
proposals.rationale: Complex/long tasks show weak upfront planning. Mandate explicit decomposition + risk identification.

proposals.after: Always emit: DECISION: <why>, VERIF: <result>, RECOVERY: <what+why>, OUTCOME: <class>. Use consistent prefixes for easy parsing by PRM/flywheel.
proposals.before: Minimal or absent structured logs of decisions, verifications, recoveries.
proposals.rationale: Rich signals (notes, errors, PRM) exist but agent observability is weak; stronger logs enable better future PRM + debugging.

proposals.after: On error: CLASSIFY <12 words (transient/auth/schema/timeout/rate-limit/permanent/unknown). Route: transient->retry once; auth/schema->escalate with context; permanent->fail fast. Log class always.
proposals.before: All errors treated identically; no routing.
proposals.rationale: 94 failures with raw errors. Mandate lightweight classification to route recovery correctly.

proposals.after: For each major action declare expected OUTPUT_CONTRACT (keys|nonempty|type|bounds) in 1 line. After call: assert vs contract (1-line). Fail contract -> immediate recovery.
proposals.rationale: Result shapes vary or low-PRM post-action steps. Enforce lightweight contracts for verification.

proposals.after: Transient errors: exponential backoff (base 1.5s, max 3 attempts, jitter). Log attempt#. Permanent errors: no retry. Use for tool+http classes in error_taxonomy.
proposals.before: Immediate retry or unbounded loops on transient.
proposals.estimated_delta: lower cost + fewer quota hits on transients
proposals.rationale: Transient errors or rate/timeout patterns. Add explicit backoff to avoid thundering herd or quota burn.

source: agentforge-runner/flywheel-step (pure Rust, Phase1-max + split_basic_LLM_critique_path + 15+ conditional high-value sections)
